headline: USC holds off Glass and Oklahoma in high-wire offensive shootout
body: Arnold Glass put on a clinic Sunday that would ordinarily demand a victory lap. The Oklahoma quarterback completed 30 of 46 passes for 388 yards and three touchdowns, orchestrating an offense that generated 445 yards and kept USC's defense honest for sixty minutes. Yet Glass and the Sooners walked out of Los Angeles on the short end of a 35-32 decision, a bitter pill made all the more bitter by how close they came to stealing a road win in the Pac.

The real difference lived in the air. USC's Ben Moss, working from a shakier line and facing consistent pressure from Oklahoma's front four, still managed 439 passing yards and two touchdowns on 25 completions. More critically, Wes Burnett became the focal point of an otherwise fragmented Trojan receiving corps, catching eight passes for 201 yards and a score. That kind of volume output against Oklahoma's secondary, which had no answer for vertical routes into the intermediate zones, proved decisive in a game decided by a field goal.

Oklahoma's defense, anchored by Tyler Fiddler's steady seven tackles, never truly broke, but neither could they manufacture a turnover when one mattered most. The Sooners committed only one turnover themselves, a fumble that could have swung field position late. The USC defense got after Glass just enough—defensive end Derek Johnson's two sacks late in the third quarter forced Oklahoma into long third downs at critical junctures—and that marginal advantage in situational football tipped the scales.
turningPoint: Burnett's 43-yard reception down the right sideline with 5:47 remaining set up a USC field goal that stretched the lead to five, a margin Glass could not overcome despite a final drive that moved the Sooners into field goal range.

unsungHero.name: Wes Burnett
unsungHero.why: Glass earned Player of the Game for his volume and touchdowns, but Burnett's 201 yards and uncovered routes were the structural reason USC won. He was the game's most impactful receiver and the focal point USC leaned on when Oklahoma tightened coverage elsewhere.
